summary: Core flows are strong: full CRUD holds across every resource kind, fork/push/pull versioning behaves correctly with version_created signals and ref cascading, dirty-workspace gates refuse pull and remove with clear kinds/hints, run lifecycle (start/view/stop/remove) cleans run plans atomically, and top-level discoverability from --help is solid. Failures cluster around error-quality and write-path hygiene: `create` without `--name` and `run tell` with missing/unknown recipients bounce `internal` 500s instead of validation errors; `edit` with no flags silently bumps versions; deleting a referenced skill succeeds and silently empties the team's refs rather than guarding or cascading; a `push` conflict emits a hint (`clier pull @clier/greeting-prompt`) that dead-ends at `working_copy_missing` because bare instructions have no working copy; uppercase names are silently lowercased without warning; `pull --force` reports `already_up_to_date` even when it discards local dirty edits.

findings.title: clier version/auth/OS captured
findings.rationale: clier --version returned `dev`; auth status returned {"login":"jakeraft"}; host is Darwin 24.5.0.

findings.title: --help is complete across commands and subcommands
findings.rationale: Ran --help on every top-level command (create, get, list, edit, delete, fork, versions, clone, fetch, pull, push, status, remove, run, auth, config, org, open, tutorial) and on every subcommand. Each returns a usage block with flags, examples, and cross-refs. Tutorial narrates the full fork→clone→edit→push→pull loop.

references.label: clier --help
references.excerpt: Resources: create/delete/edit/fork/get/list/versions. Runtime: run. Workspace: clone/fetch/pull/push/remove/status. Settings: auth/config/open/org/tutorial.

references.label: clier tutorial
references.excerpt: Phase 1 Steps 1–10 (canned team); Phase 2 Steps 11–19 (fork it and iterate)

findings.title: Skill create → get → edit → delete leaves no residue
findings.rationale: Create returned latest_version:1; edit with new content returned latest_version:2 and version_created:true; delete returned {"deleted":...}; follow-up get returned kind=resource_not_found with exit 1.

steps.label: create
steps.command: clier create skill --name qa-20260418153748-skill --content '# Skill\n\nTest content.'

findings.title: Instruction create → get → edit → delete clean
findings.rationale: Same CRUD shape as skill; edit bumped to v2 with version_created:true; delete succeeded.

findings.title: claude-settings CRUD round-trips
findings.rationale: Created with JSON content, edit extended the permissions list, delete succeeded. kind rendered as `claude-setting` (singular) consistently.

findings.title: codex-settings CRUD round-trips
findings.rationale: Created with TOML content (`model = "gpt-5"`), edited to `gpt-5-codex`, deleted. kind rendered as `codex-setting`.

findings.title: Team create with skill + instruction refs
findings.rationale: Team was created with --skill and --instruction pinned at @1; get returned a populated refs array containing both. Edit (summary-only) cascaded to v2. Delete of team succeeded; leaves cleaned separately.

findings.title: Fork, versions, @pin, and invalid-version query are all well-behaved
findings.rationale: fork @clier/hello-codex created jakeraft/hello-codex at v1 with refs preserved; versions returned an items array; get @1 returned the v1 snapshot; get @999 returned resource_version_not_found with kind+subject.

findings.title: Fork rejects @version targets with actionable message
findings.rationale: `clier fork @clier/hello-codex@1` returned kind=invalid_argument with message 'expected <owner/name> without @<version>'. Matches --help: 'fork only accepts <owner/name>'.

findings.title: Local edit → push bumps leaf and cascades team
findings.rationale: Appended marker to jakeraft.hello-codex/AGENTS.md. `clier status` shifted the greeting-prompt tracked row to local=modified. `clier push` returned two entries: instruction @clier/greeting-prompt 1→2 reason=local_edit, and team jakeraft/hello-codex 1→2 reason=ref_cascade. Follow-up status returned local=clean with pinned_version advanced.

findings.title: Dirty workspace refuses pull and remove with clear kinds
findings.rationale: With AGENTS.md locally modified, pull returned kind=pull_blocked_dirty (hint: push or revert before pulling) and remove returned kind=workspace_dirty (hint: push or revert before removing). Both cited the modified file count.

findings.title: status reports pin_outdated when server leaf is newer than team's ref
findings.rationale: After bumping @clier/greeting-prompt to v3 server-side, `clier fetch` and `clier pull` correctly said 'already_up_to_date' (the team itself is not behind), while `clier status` surfaced pin_outdated:1 with hint 'team still pins this version; edit team to upgrade' on the greeting-prompt row. The CLI distinguishes 'team up-to-date at its pinned refs' from 'a referenced leaf has advanced'.

findings.title: push refuses when leaf's remote version advanced since pin
findings.rationale: With greeting-prompt pinned at v2 and server at v3, a push of a new local edit returned kind=remote_changed with name/owner/resource_kind subject.

findings.title: push `remote_changed` hints a command that resolves to working_copy_missing
findings.rationale: The push error hint is literally `'clier pull @clier/greeting-prompt'`, but running that command returns kind=working_copy_missing with hint 'run clier clone @clier/greeting-prompt first' — and clone on an instruction returns kind=not_team_resource. The hint therefore chains to a dead-end for an agent trying to follow trust-point 3 ('failure guides next action'). The correct recovery is to pull the team (`clier pull jakeraft/hello-codex`) after reverting local edits, or to accept the leaf bump via team edit.
findings.fail_note: Hint should name the working copy (`clier pull jakeraft/hello-codex`) — or the CLI should auto-resolve the containing working copy — rather than a leaf ref that has no clone.
findings.stderr_excerpt: hint: pull before pushing: 'clier pull @clier/greeting-prompt'  → working_copy_missing

findings.title: pull --force silently discards local edits and still reports already_up_to_date
findings.rationale: With an unapproved `DIRTY-MARK` line in AGENTS.md, `clier pull --force jakeraft/hello-codex` returned {"status":"already_up_to_date","resources":[]} and the file was reverted to the pinned content. The result is correct (nothing new to pull; --force reverted the dirty diff), but the output does not mention that local edits were discarded. Passing because the pin-level claim is accurate, with the transparency gap noted.

findings.title: run start/view/stop and remove cascade are consistent
findings.rationale: run start returned {run_id,session}; run view exposed status:running with agents/messages/notes arrays ([] not null). Duplicate start returned kind=run_already_running with hint to stop first. remove while running returned kind=run_blocks_remove. After stop, view showed status:stopped with stopped_at; remove cascaded cleanly returning removed_runs.

findings.title: run note inherits CLIER_RUN_ID; run tell delivers to a valid recipient
findings.rationale: `clier run note` without --run posted with agent/run fields populated from env. `clier run tell --to @clier/clier-qa-claude` returned {"status":"delivered",...} for a self-message.

findings.title: create skill without --name returns internal 500
findings.rationale: `clier create skill --content x` (no --name) returned kind=internal with the generic message 'the server encountered an internal error'. Missing required input should be rejected with kind=invalid_argument naming the offending field, matching how other validation errors behave (e.g., `--name 'bad name'` correctly returns invalid_argument).
findings.fail_note: Validate --name client-side or return kind=invalid_argument with subject={name:required}. An internal 500 is both misleading and hides the true cause for agents parsing error.kind.

findings.title: edit with no changes silently creates a new identical version
findings.rationale: `clier edit jakeraft/<skill>` with no flags returned version_created:true and latest_version:2 even though spec.content did not change (v1 content == v2 content). This pollutes the version history and lets an agent bump versions accidentally by re-issuing the same command.
findings.fail_note: Either reject edits that carry no fields with invalid_argument, or make the create-new-version path content-hash aware (no-op when unchanged).

findings.title: Deleting a referenced skill succeeds and silently empties the team's refs
findings.rationale: Created team `qa-20260418153748-refteam` pinning skill `qa-20260418153748-refskill@1`; the skill's ref_count correctly showed 1. `clier delete jakeraft/qa-20260418153748-refskill` returned {"deleted":...} with exit 0. A subsequent `clier get` on the team returned refs:[] — the pinned reference was silently dropped rather than preserved or blocked. This violates trust-point 4 (state transparency) and trust-point 9 (ref-graph integrity): the team's v1 snapshot no longer matches what get returns.
findings.fail_note: Either block delete when ref_count>0 (`resource_in_use` with a list of dependents), or cascade-bump dependent teams to a new version and report the impact. Silent ref removal means an agent can't trust `get` to describe the team that was actually pushed.

findings.title: run tell without --to returns internal 500 instead of invalid_argument
findings.rationale: `clier run tell --run <id> "msg"` (no --to) returned kind=internal. Missing a required flag should be a client-side validation error or kind=invalid_argument with subject={to:required}.
findings.fail_note: Validate --to before round-tripping to the server, or return invalid_argument. Pairs with create-without-name in the same 500-instead-of-validation bucket.

findings.title: run tell to a non-member agent returns kind=internal despite clear subject
findings.rationale: `clier run tell --run <id> --to jakeraft/ghost 'probe'` returned kind=internal with subject.detail='agent jakeraft/ghost not found in run plan'. The underlying diagnosis is correct and actionable, but kind=internal hides it from consumers that branch on error.kind.
findings.fail_note: Surface this as kind=invalid_argument or kind=agent_not_in_run (new), keeping the same subject/hint. This is a user-input error, not a server failure.
findings.stdout_excerpt: "kind":"internal",..."detail":"agent jakeraft/ghost not found in run plan"

findings.title: create with uppercase name is silently normalized
findings.rationale: `clier create skill --name BAD-CASE --content x` returned exit 0 with metadata.name="bad-case". By contrast, `--name 'bad name'` (space) is correctly rejected with invalid_argument. The CLI applies inconsistent rules to name input: some invalid characters are rejected, capitalization is silently mutated. An agent that later `get`s `jakeraft/BAD-CASE` would fail to find it.
findings.fail_note: Either reject uppercase with invalid_argument (matching the treatment of spaces), or echo a normalized name and document it. Silent transformation is the surprising case.

findings.title: Nonexistent-resource paths return structured, specific errors
findings.rationale: clone/edit/delete/get/versions on jakeraft/qa-20260418153748-nope all returned kind=resource_not_found with subject carrying name/owner/resource_kind. fork returned the same. list --uses on a nonexistent target returned an empty items array (intentional filter semantics).

findings.title: org and config commands reject invalid input cleanly
findings.rationale: `clier org create @qa-foo` returned invalid_argument 'name: must not contain @'. `clier config set workspace-dir relative/path` returned kind=workspace_dir_not_absolute with subject.path echoed back. `clier list --kind foo` returned invalid_argument 'kind: invalid value'.

findings.title: JSON output is consistent across commands (snake_case, [] not null, RFC3339)
findings.rationale: Every command emitted a single JSON object or wrapped {items,total} collection on stdout. Empty collections rendered as [] (messages, notes, refs, runs all verified). Timestamps use Z suffix for server times and the host offset for run start/stop times, matching the --help output conventions.

findings.title: Disposable prefix cleanup is complete
findings.rationale: Final `clier list --mine --limit 50` returned items:[] (total:0). All qa-20260418153748-* resources and the jakeraft/hello-codex fork were deleted; the jakeraft.hello-codex working copy and its run plan were removed.
